back: A software component is a modular, deployable, and replaceable part of a system that encapsulates its implementation and exposes a set of interfaces. It is a self-contained unit of functionality that can be composed with other components to build larger applications.

front: What are the key characteristics of a well-designed component?
back: High cohesion (all parts are strongly related), low coupling (minimal dependencies on other components), well-defined interfaces, substitutability (can be replaced by another component with the same interface), and reusability.

front: What is the primary goal of Component-Based Software Engineering (CBSE)?
back: The primary goal of CBSE is to assemble systems from pre-existing, reusable, and interoperable components, rather than building them from scratch. This aims to reduce development time and cost while increasing software quality and reliability.

front: What is the difference between a component and a module?
back: A module is a code-based construct (like a class or package) used for compile-time and build-time organization. A component is a run-time entity that is independently deployable and has explicit context dependencies. All components are modules, but not all modules are components.

front: What is a component model? Provide an common example.
back: A component model defines standards and specifications for implementing, deploying, and managing components. It specifies how interfaces are defined and how components communicate. A common example is the Enterprise JavaBeans (EJB) model for server-side components in Java.
